verdict_reason: The single disagreement entry on katarat ... garīyaḥ names the doctrinal axis precisely: garīyaḥ literally means 'heavier/weightier' (cognate with English 'grave'), translators flatten to 'better' or 'preferable,' and the literal sense is doctrinally consequential — 'Arjuna is not asking which is morally better, he is asking which carries more weight. The two outcomes feel equally consequential and equally awful, which is precisely the symptom of the framing being wrong.' This identifies the philological axis (literal vs interpretive register), the translator pattern (flattening), and the doctrinal stake (the symmetric weight is itself the diagnostic). One tight line of high-quality explanation. Score 9, PASS.
